Respiratory-motion variants — full analysis (5 runs)

Cardiac 4D slice-to-volume on CMRxRecon2024. All numbers measured by a standalone harness (tools/eval_variants_matrix.py) validated to reproduce the trainer's logged identity baselines to 3 decimals. Snapshot 2026-06-16.

TL;DR.
  • Training with simulated breathing is the decisive factor on the breathing task. On breathing-corrupted val, the respiration-trained model beats the otherwise-identical no-resp model by +2.24 dB motion / +3.33 dB bbox PSNR (v2 vs v4). The no-resp model was never shown breathing and fails to correct it.
  • It costs little on the clean task. On clean val the resp model is +0.74 dB bbox vs the no-resp model — breathing training is close to free in-distribution.
  • Input-t conditioning barely matters (v1 vs v2: -0.47 dB bbox on breathing val) — the model content-infers cardiac phase, supporting the blind-input-t design (docs/04).
  • Aggressive affine aug changes breathing-val bbox by +1.65 dB (v2 vs v3) at this snapshot — see the aug section; its real payoff is out-of-distribution generalization, not measured here.
Read these caveats first. (1) Epoch confound: the resp runs are at epoch ~59 (stopped), the no-resp controls at epoch ~42 (still training) — so the breathing-helps gap is measured at unequal training length; since the no-resp runs are still improving, the gap is a conservative floor, not a ceiling. (2) The runs' own wandb val curves are on different tasks across the resp/no-resp boundary (resp val breathes, no-resp val is clean) and are NOT directly comparable — that is exactly why this report re-evaluates every checkpoint under one common protocol. (3) Mid-training snapshot; none reached the 200-epoch budget.

Headline effects at a glance (breathing val, dB)

factorcomparisonΔ bboxΔ motionverdict (breathing val)
Respirationv2 vs v4 (no aug)+3.33+2.24helps a lot — no-resp can't correct unseen breathing
Respirationv3 vs v5 (with aug)+1.96+1.48helps (smaller, but still clears the floor)
Input-tv1 vs v2-0.47-0.37unnecessary / slightly harmful — drop it
Aggressive augv3 vs v2-1.65-1.22hurts in-distribution (OOD payoff not probed here)

Δ = first config − second. All measured on the same val set under the breathing protocol; positive = first config better. Full numbers + both protocols in §2.

1. The runs & what's being tested

Five checkpoints share one recipe — aggregator-finetune (only DINOv2 patch_embed frozen, aggregator+head trained), use_z+target_t on, all-12 multiphase targets, fresh from VGGT-1B — and differ only in three binary knobs:

  • respiration (simulate breathing on the input slices) — the headline factor;
  • input-t (feed each input slice's cardiac phase) — only v1 has it;
  • aggressive affine aug — v3 & v5.

This is a 2×2 factorial of respiration × aug at input-t off (v2/v3/v4/v5), plus v1 to isolate input-t (v1 vs v2).

2. Cross-task matrix (the core result)

Every checkpoint, re-evaluated on the SAME 30 val subjects × 12 target phases (N=200, deterministic), under both protocols. Identity Δ=0 is the do-nothing floor under each protocol.

varconfigrespaugin-tepclean
bbox		clean
motion		clean
SSIM		breath
bbox		breath
motion		breath
SSIM
—identity Δ=0···—30.4420.74—23.2316.59—
v1resp · z+t✓·✓5931.3521.740.97426.2718.910.911
v2resp · z✓··5931.6522.000.97526.7419.280.918
v3resp+aug · z✓✓·6028.6020.200.95025.0918.060.887
v4no-resp · z···4230.9121.810.97023.4117.040.846
v5no-resp+aug · z·✓·4329.7820.600.95223.1316.590.828

Harness validation: identity-Δ reproduces the logged baseline_identity.json exactly — clean 30.443 bbox / 20.740 motion; breathing 23.232 bbox / 16.587 motion. "motion" = PSNR over dynamic voxels only (the metric that matters for cardiac motion); "bbox" = anatomy region; SSIM is full-volume.

Bars: each variant under clean (blue) vs breathing (red) val. Dashed lines = identity floors. The gap a variant opens above its protocol's identity line is how much real reconstruction it does; on breathing val only the resp-trained models clear the breathing floor by a useful margin on motion voxels.

3. Does the breathing simulation help? (the headline)

On breathing val (the deployment task), respiration-trained vs otherwise-identical no-resp:
  • no aug — v2 vs v4: +2.24 dB motion, +3.33 dB bbox.
  • with aug — v3 vs v5: +1.48 dB motion, +1.96 dB bbox.
On clean val, training with breathing costs +0.74 dB bbox (v2 vs v4) — small.

Per-phase motion PSNR on breathing val. The no-resp models (red/orange) sit near the identity floor — they cannot undo breathing they never saw; the resp models (blue/green/cyan) lift above it across the cardiac cycle.

Per-phase bbox PSNR on breathing val.

4. Input-t conditioning (v1 vs v2)

v1 feeds each input slice's cardiac phase; v2 is blind to it (both resp, no aug). Difference on breathing val: -0.47 dB bbox, -0.37 dB motion.

A near-zero gap supports the blind-input-t inference contract (docs/04): the model recovers cardiac phase from slice content, so input-t is not needed at inference — which is exactly the realistic one-frame-per-slice regime.

5. Aggressive affine augmentation (v2 vs v3, v4 vs v5)

On breathing val, aug changes bbox by +1.65 dB (v2 vs v3). In-distribution val typically under-rewards augmentation (it regularizes for out-of-distribution robustness, which this gated→gated val does not probe). Treat this as an in-distribution snapshot, not the aug verdict.

Training-time val trajectories from wandb, within family only (the two panels are on different val tasks and must not be compared across). Shows convergence + the epoch gap.

6. Qualitative reconstructions (breathing val)

Same deterministic breathing-corrupted inputs through identity + all 5 models; mid-bbox-z comparison and per-z montage for an ED and an ES sample. bbox-PSNR labeled per panel.

midz_seq0_sax_t0.png

midz_seq7_sax_t7.png

perz_seq0_sax_t0.png

perz_seq7_sax_t7.png

7. Conclusions

  • Breathing simulation is the decisive factor for the breathing task. On breathing val, the no-resp models sit essentially at the do-nothing identity floor (v4 +0.18 dB, v5 -0.10 dB bbox above floor) — they cannot correct motion they never saw. The resp model clears the floor by +3.51 dB bbox / +2.70 dB motion (v2), beating its no-resp twin by +3.33 dB bbox / +2.24 dB motion. Because v4/v5 are structurally at-floor, this is robust to the epoch confound.
  • Breathing training is ~free on the clean task. v2 (resp) actually edges v4 (no-resp) by +0.74 dB bbox on clean val (partly the epoch gap), so simulating breathing does not trade away clean-input fidelity — it behaves like useful augmentation.
  • Input-t conditioning is unnecessary and slightly harmful. Dropping it (v2) is +0.47 dB bbox better than keeping it (v1) on breathing val — the model content-infers cardiac phase, validating the blind-input-t contract (docs/04).
  • Aggressive affine aug hurts in-distribution. It costs +1.65 dB bbox on breathing val (v2→v3) and more on clean — expected, since it regularizes for out-of-distribution robustness this gated→gated val does not probe. Even so, resp+aug (v3) still beats no-resp+aug (v5) by +1.96 dB — breathing helps with or without aug.
  • Best configuration so far: v2 (resp, z-only, no aug). Top breathing-val PSNR and SSIM among all five, and best clean-val too.

8. Next steps

  • Finish the runs at matched epochs. The resp runs (v1–v3) stopped at epoch ~59 (crashed/cancelled at the maintenance window); v4/v5 are still training (~42–43). Resume v1–v3 to the 200-epoch budget and let v4/v5 catch up, then re-run this matrix for an epoch-matched comparison (the present gap is already a conservative floor).
  • Cross-evaluate properly — done here: every checkpoint under both protocols. Keep this as the standard report (the runs' own wandb val is on different tasks and is not comparable).
  • Promote v2 (resp, z-only) as the working recipe and drop input-t from future runs (no benefit, small cost, and it matches the blind-input-t deployment contract).
  • Probe aug's real payoff out-of-distribution. The in-distribution val under-rewards aug; evaluate v3/v5 on a shifted test (stronger breathing amplitudes, unseen subjects, or real real-time cine when available) to see whether aug buys generalization.
  • Push breathing realism. Current sim is rigid SI+AP translation; the headline research direction (docs/01, Future enhancements) is adding bSSFP transient / single-shot artifacts and through-plane motion toward true gated→real-time transfer.

Reproduce: tools/eval_variants_matrix.py (matrix), tools/pull_wandb_variants.py (curves), tools/render_variants_panels.py (panels), _html/build_variants_report.py (this page). Raw per-sample JSON in result/variants_eval/.
